tasks.name: Copy a Node.js code sample for creating a project with a specific name
tasks.description: Navigate the API documentation to find the POST /projects endpoint, switch the code sample to Node.js, update the example payload so the project name is 'Sample Project A', and copy the modified code using the built-in copy button.
steps: From the documentation homepage, click the main navigation link labeled 'API Reference' or similar to open the API reference section.
In the left-hand sidebar of the API reference, locate and click the 'Projects' resource or section name.
Within the 'Projects' section, find the list of endpoints and click the endpoint whose HTTP method is 'POST' and whose path is '/projects'.
On the endpoint detail page, locate the code samples area and click the language tab labeled 'Node.js' to select it.
Scroll within the Node.js code sample until you see the JSON payload body containing a 'name' field.
Click inside the value of the 'name' field in the JSON payload and replace its current value with the exact text 'Sample Project A'.
After editing the payload, locate the button or icon near the Node.js code sample labeled 'Copy' or showing a copy icon.
Click the 'Copy' button or copy icon to copy the modified Node.js code sample that creates a project named 'Sample Project A'.

tasks.name: Use the Try-It console to list users created after a specific date with a fixed page size
tasks.description: Find the GET /users endpoint, open its interactive console, and configure a request that lists users created after 2023-01-01 with a per_page value of 25, then execute the request from within the documentation.
steps: From the documentation homepage, use the top navigation and click on 'API Reference' or the equivalent section link.
In the sidebar of the API reference, locate and click on the 'Users' section or resource group.
Within the 'Users' section, click on the endpoint with HTTP method 'GET' and path '/users' that lists users.
On the GET /users endpoint page, scroll until you find the 'Try It' or 'API Explorer' panel for making example requests.
In the parameters area of the Try-It console, locate the query parameter field labeled 'created_after' and enter the exact date value '2023-01-01'.
In the same parameters area, locate the query parameter field labeled 'per_page' and enter the numeric value '25'.
If there is a request method selector, make sure 'GET' is selected, then locate the button labeled 'Send', 'Run', or similar inside the Try-It console.
Click the 'Send' or 'Run' button to execute the configured GET /users request with created_after set to 2023-01-01 and per_page set to 25.

tasks.name: Compare order details endpoint behavior between v1 and v2 API versions
tasks.description: Find the GET /orders/{id} endpoint in the API reference, switch between v1 and v2 versions using the version selector, and open the v2 version that includes an 'include' query parameter supporting the value 'items'.
steps: From the documentation homepage, click on the 'API Reference' link in the main navigation bar.
In the API reference sidebar, locate and click the 'Orders' resource or section name.
Within the 'Orders' section, find and click the endpoint with HTTP method 'GET' and path pattern '/orders/{id}'.
On the GET /orders/{id} endpoint page, locate the version selector control (for example, a dropdown labeled 'API Version').
Use the version selector to choose 'v1' and briefly scroll through the parameters section to identify the available query parameters.
Use the same version selector to switch from 'v1' to 'v2' for the GET /orders/{id} endpoint.
In the v2 endpoint view, scroll to the parameters table and locate the query parameter named 'include' that supports additional related data.
Click on or highlight the row or description for the 'include' parameter and focus on the option that mentions 'items' as an allowed value for this parameter.

tasks.name: Identify the documented rate limit per minute for the payments creation endpoint
tasks.description: Navigate to the rate limiting documentation, find the table entry for the POST /payments endpoint, and focus on the value that shows the per-minute rate limit applied to this endpoint.
steps: From the documentation homepage, click the navigation link labeled 'Guides', 'Concepts', or similar high-level documentation section.
Within that section, locate and click the page titled 'Rate Limits', 'Rate Limiting', or a similar heading related to request limits.
On the rate limit documentation page, scroll down until you find a table or list that describes rate limits for specific endpoints.
In the rate limits table, scan the first column for an entry that references the endpoint path 'POST /payments' or similar payments creation endpoint.
Click or tap on the row for 'POST /payments' if it is expandable, or position the view so the full row is visible on the screen.
Within that row, locate the column that indicates the limit per minute (for example, 'Requests per minute' or 'RPM').
Focus on the numeric value in the per-minute column for the 'POST /payments' row and mentally note this as the documented limit.
Keep the rate limits table visible, with the 'POST /payments' row and its per-minute value centered in the viewport for reference.

tasks.name: Open and copy the example payload for the 'invoice.paid' webhook event
tasks.description: Use the webhooks documentation to find the 'invoice.paid' event, switch to the raw JSON example payload view if necessary, and copy the example payload using the provided copy button.
steps: From the documentation homepage, click the navigation link labeled 'Webhooks' or a similarly titled section about webhook events.
On the Webhooks overview page, scroll to find and click the subsection or link titled 'Invoice events', 'Invoices', or similar.
Within the invoice-related webhooks section, locate the specific event labeled 'invoice.paid' in the list of events.
Click on the 'invoice.paid' event entry to open its detailed documentation page or expand its details panel.
On the 'invoice.paid' event details, scroll until you find the example payload or example delivery section.
If there is a set of tabs for formats (such as 'JSON', 'cURL', 'Payload'), click the tab that shows the raw JSON payload example.
Locate the 'Copy' button or copy icon associated with the JSON payload example for the 'invoice.paid' event.
Click the 'Copy' button or icon to copy the complete example JSON payload for the 'invoice.paid' webhook event.

tasks.name: Locate the Python SDK method for deleting a customer by ID
tasks.description: Navigate to the SDK documentation, open the Python SDK reference, find the Customers class or module, and open the example that deletes a customer given a customer_id argument.
steps: From the documentation homepage, click on the link in the main navigation labeled 'SDKs', 'Client Libraries', or similar.
On the SDKs overview page, locate the Python SDK card or link and click it to open the Python-specific documentation.
Inside the Python SDK docs, find the sidebar or contents list and click on the section labeled 'Customers' or an equivalent customer-related module.
Within the Customers section, scan the list of available methods and click the method whose name indicates a delete action, such as 'delete_customer' or 'delete'.
On the method detail page, scroll to the code example that shows how to delete a customer using the Python client.
In the example code, locate the line where the delete method is called and identify the argument representing the customer ID (for example, 'customer_id').
Click and drag across the delete example code block to select it if needed, or position the view so the delete call is fully visible.
If available, click the 'Copy' button next to the Python delete example so that the code call including the customer_id argument is copied.

tasks.name: Copy a cURL example that uses API key authentication in the Authorization header
tasks.description: Open the authentication documentation, navigate to the API key authentication section, and copy the cURL example that sends an Authorization header with a Bearer-style API key.
steps: From the documentation homepage, click the main navigation link labeled 'Authentication' or a similarly named section.
On the authentication overview page, locate and click the subsection or tab labeled 'API Key', 'Bearer Token', or equivalent.
Within the API key authentication section, scroll down until you find a code example that uses cURL.
Click on the language selector or tabs for the example and choose 'cURL' if it is not already selected.
In the cURL example, visually locate the header line that includes the word 'Authorization' and the 'Bearer' scheme alongside a placeholder API key.
Position the viewport so that the full cURL command, including the Authorization header, is clearly visible.
Find the 'Copy' button or copy icon associated with this cURL example command.
Click the 'Copy' button or icon to copy the cURL command that includes the Authorization: Bearer <api_key> header.

tasks.name: Configure a paginated request for pending orders using the Try-It console
tasks.description: Find the GET /orders endpoint, open the Try-It console, and execute a request for pending orders with per_page set to 50, page set to 3, and sorting by created_at in descending order.
steps: From the documentation homepage, click the 'API Reference' link in the top navigation bar.
In the API reference sidebar, locate and click on the 'Orders' resource or section name.
Within the 'Orders' section, click the endpoint with HTTP method 'GET' and path '/orders' that lists orders.
On the GET /orders endpoint page, scroll to the 'Try It' or 'API Explorer' panel where you can configure example requests.
In the parameters area of the Try-It console, find the query parameter labeled 'status' and enter the value 'pending'.
Locate the query parameter field labeled 'per_page' and enter the numeric value '50', then locate the 'page' field and enter the numeric value '3'.
Find the query parameter or option labeled 'sort' (or similar) and enter or select the value that corresponds to 'created_at' in descending order (for example, '-created_at').

With these parameters set, click the 'Send' or 'Run' button in the Try-It console to execute the GET /orders request for page 3 of pending orders with 50 results per page sorted by created_at descending.

entities.name: Page
entities.description: Top-level documentation page such as Home, API Reference, Rate Limits, Webhooks Overview, SDKs, Authentication, etc.

entities.name: NavigationLink

fields.name: url
fields.description: Href used in the link, e.g. api_reference.html

entities.description: Link shown in the global header or footer navigation (e.g., Home, API Reference, Guides, Webhooks, SDKs, Authentication).

entities.name: ApiResource
entities.description: Logical group of API endpoints, as shown in the API Reference sidebar (Projects, Users, Orders, Payments, etc.).

entities.name: ApiEndpoint
entities.description: Endpoint definition attached to a resource, such as POST /projects, GET /users, GET /orders, GET /orders/{id}, POST /payments.

entities.name: ApiEndpointVersion

fields.name: docsPageId
fields.description: Typically the API Reference Page id

entities.description: Version-specific endpoint definition (e.g., GET /orders/{id} v1 vs v2) used for version selector and parameter differences.

entities.name: ApiParameter

fields.name: allowedValues
fields.description: Optional list of allowed values for enum-like parameters, e.g. include=['items'] or status=['pending']

entities.description: Parameter definition for an endpoint version, including query, path, header, and body parameters used in Try-It consoles and docs tables.

entities.name: ApiCodeSample

fields.name: code
fields.description: Full code text including any JSON payloads (e.g., POST /projects body with a 'name' field).

entities.description: Language-specific code sample for an API endpoint version, including editable Node.js samples and copyable cURL examples.

entities.name: TryItConsole

fields.name: methodSelectorEnabled
fields.description: Whether the HTTP method can be changed in the console UI

fields.name: defaultQueryParams
fields.description: Optional list of default query params in 'key=value' form

entities.description: Configuration for the interactive Try-It / API Explorer console attached to an endpoint version (e.g., GET /users, GET /orders).

entities.name: RateLimitRule

fields.name: endpointId
fields.description: Linked when scope is 'endpoint', e.g. POST /payments endpoint id

fields.name: path
fields.description: Endpoint path this rule applies to, e.g. '/payments'

fields.name: limitPerTimeWindow
fields.description: Numeric limit, e.g. requests per minute

entities.description: Represents a row in the rate limit documentation table, including the per-minute limit for specific endpoints such as POST /payments.

entities.name: WebhookEventGroup

fields.name: pageId
fields.description: Page id for the group, e.g. Webhooks – Invoice Events page

entities.description: Grouping of webhook events in the docs, such as the Invoice events group that contains invoice.paid.

entities.name: WebhookEvent
entities.description: Specific webhook event such as invoice.paid, invoice.created, etc., with its own detail section in the docs.

entities.name: WebhookPayloadExample

fields.name: payloadJson
fields.description: Raw JSON text of the example webhook payload

entities.description: Example payload/delivery for a webhook event, such as the raw JSON payload for 'invoice.paid', with an associated copy control.

entities.name: Sdk

fields.name: pageId
fields.description: Language-specific SDK docs Page id, e.g. Python SDK page

entities.description: Represents an official client library such as the Python SDK, with its own documentation page.

entities.name: SdkModule
entities.description: Logical module within an SDK documentation (e.g., Customers, Orders, Payments for the Python SDK).

entities.name: SdkMethod

fields.name: apiEndpointId
fields.description: Optional linked ApiEndpoint id that this SDK method wraps

fields.name: idParameterName
fields.description: Name of the ID argument, e.g. 'customer_id'

entities.description: Individual SDK method such as the Python Customers.delete_customer method that deletes a customer by ID.

entities.name: SdkCodeExample

fields.name: code
fields.description: Example code snippet invoking the SDK method (e.g., delete_customer(customer_id='...'))

entities.description: Code examples for SDK methods, including the Python delete-customer example with a customer_id argument and copy control.

entities.name: AuthMethod

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name, e.g. 'API Key / Bearer Token'

entities.description: Authentication scheme documented on the Authentication page, such as API key or Bearer token usage.

entities.name: AuthCodeExample

fields.name: code
fields.description: Command or snippet showing authenticated usage (e.g., cURL with Authorization: Bearer <api_key>).

entities.description: Language-specific authentication examples, including the cURL example that sends an Authorization: Bearer <api_key> header with a copy control.
